---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 7a07ee0f4dbb047996b1775731e4f4885be4b6f7
findings:
  - { id: judged-pf-1, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's attestation obligation requires every disclosed path be 'produced by the real path-construction helpers … never hand-typed or re-derived independently'. The attestation path honors this (evidence.AttestationPath), but two other disclosed paths are independently re-derived string joins: (a) the derived-tree root — closepreflight.go:190,195 re-joins \".verdi/data/derived/\u003cRefSlug\u003e\" instead of sharing foldload.go:36's own inline join, and (b) the deviation-report.md path — closepreflight.go:175 re-joins \".verdi/specs/active/\u003cname\u003e/deviation-report.md\" separately from checkSpecStaleCondition's own join (closuregate.go:110, and a third copy in internal/wallbadge/ladder.go:61). Both copies currently match byte-for-byte, but nothing structural forces agreement — the exact 'two predicates that can drift' pattern dc-1's second judge fix eliminated for the CI-guard disclosure ('one predicate, two print sites') is left open here: if the fold's read location or the gate's report path ever moves, preflight would keep naming the stale path while the tests (which hand-join the same expectation strings) still pass. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the two disclosed diagnostic paths match byte-for-byte (judge admits); no wrong output. Pre-existing and WIDESPREAD, not introduced by this fix: the derived-tree join recurs at ~8 cmd/verdi sites (baseline/foldload/rollup/closefeature/featurematrix/...) and the deviation-report join at ~6 (align/closuregate/gate/disposition/wallbadge); centralizing them into a shared path helper is a cross-cutting code-health refactor beyond this focused three-finding remediation. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.45 exceeds the sub-0.30 accept-expectation — flagged for controller review / a separate code-health pass." }
  - { id: judged-pf-2, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 requires each defect-class pair to run both halves 'asserting the refusal reason matches, not merely that it also fails'. The unauthored-attestation-scaffold subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts preflight's disclosure text but on the real-close side asserts only the exit code (gotClose != 1), with no assertion that close's refusal reason is the same eligibility condition — unlike every sibling subtest, which checks the matching '[FAIL] closure: 1. story eligible' (or reason-string) line. For this one defect class the agreement property is proven only as 'it also fails', which is exactly what ac-3's text forbids relying on. (confidence 0.40)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the real close DOES refuse for the eligibility reason (an unauthored scaffold is not foldable); only this PRE-EXISTING unauthored subtest's close-side assertion is weaker (exit-code only) than its siblings'. The three NEW ADJ-56 agreement scenarios (findings 1/2/3) each pair the disclosure with close's matching refusal reason, so the dispatch's ac-3 extension mandate is met. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.40 exceeds the sub-0.30 accept-expectation — flagged for controller review." }
  - { id: judged-pf-3, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's non-mutation evidence is claimed as 'a working-tree diff/git status assertion proving zero mutation', but snapshotRepo (closepreflight_test.go:183-189) captures only HEAD, branch, branch list, and `git status --porcelain`. Porcelain names untracked files but not their contents, so a preflight code path that rewrote an existing untracked file — e.g. the verdicts.json files the fixtures write under .verdi/data/derived/ after the commit, precisely the tree the fold reads — would leave the snapshot byte-identical and pass every non-mutation assertion. The implementation is in fact read-only, but the test's 'proving zero mutation' claim is weaker than stated in the one directory preflight actually touches. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the judge confirms the preflight is read-only; only the pre-existing shared snapshotRepo helper's non-mutation proof is weaker (git status --porcelain names untracked files but not their bytes) than ac-2's stated claim. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.30 is AT (not below) the sub-0.30 line — flagged for controller review." }
  - { id: judged-pf-4, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's dc-1 clause says the guard disclosure prints 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'. In runPreflight (closepreflight.go:96-109) a gate error returns exit 2 before the disclosure line is reached, so an operational-error run outside CI without --force-local never prints it. This matches the clause's intent (there is no 'ready' verdict to mistake), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever' to the two verdict outcomes, undisclosed in the spec or code comments. (confidence 0.15)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Genuinely-new, non-behavioral, sub-0.30 (0.15): an operational-error (exit 2) run returns before the guard-disclosure line, a literal narrowing of dc-1's 'whenever' that matches its intent (there is no 'ready' verdict to mistake), as the judge concedes." }
  - { id: judged-pf-5, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 says the agreement pair holds 'for each defect class named in ac-1', and ac-1's feature list includes spec-stale and pending-supersession ('the same two'). Those two classes are exercised as preflight/close pairs only at story scope (closepreflight_test.go); the feature-scope tests cover floor-unmet, unreconciled-stub, and open-implementing-story only. The conditions are the literally shared checkSpecStaleCondition/checkPendingSupersessionCondition functions, so drift is structurally unlikely, but the feature-scope wiring of those two conditions (including printSpecStalePathIfFailing's path line on a feature ref) is never proven by a fixture pair. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Genuinely-new, non-behavioral, sub-0.30 (0.20): feature-scope spec-stale / pending-supersession lack a dedicated fixture pair, but they are the literally shared checkSpecStaleCondition / checkPendingSupersessionCondition proven as preflight/close pairs at story scope, so the two surfaces are structurally prevented from drifting (dc-2)." }
digest: sha256:4115ac2c2d4b1ca717d8c229b8f39c0e52e6c3f1333fb931b636f7ae29043ec5
integrity: sha256:e3e5e3f5ca7486a5a7ebc794a2ed2574644490d4b9d4af24fe5e3047739a1c10
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"pf-1\",\"text\":\"ac-1's attestation obligation requires every disclosed path be 'produced by the real path-construction helpers … never hand-typed or re-derived independently'. The attestation path honors this (evidence.AttestationPath), but two other disclosed paths are independently re-derived string joins: (a) the derived-tree root — closepreflight.go:190,195 re-joins \\\".verdi/data/derived/\u003cRefSlug\u003e\\\" instead of sharing foldload.go:36's own inline join, and (b) the deviation-report.md path — closepreflight.go:175 re-joins \\\".verdi/specs/active/\u003cname\u003e/deviation-report.md\\\" separately from checkSpecStaleCondition's own join (closuregate.go:110, and a third copy in internal/wallbadge/ladder.go:61). Both copies currently match byte-for-byte, but nothing structural forces agreement — the exact 'two predicates that can drift' pattern dc-1's second judge fix eliminated for the CI-guard disclosure ('one predicate, two print sites') is left open here: if the fold's read location or the gate's report path ever moves, preflight would keep naming the stale path while the tests (which hand-join the same expectation strings) still pass.\",\"confidence\":0.45},{\"id\":\"pf-2\",\"text\":\"ac-3 requires each defect-class pair to run both halves 'asserting the refusal reason matches, not merely that it also fails'. The unauthored-attestation-scaffold subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts preflight's disclosure text but on the real-close side asserts only the exit code (gotClose != 1), with no assertion that close's refusal reason is the same eligibility condition — unlike every sibling subtest, which checks the matching '[FAIL] closure: 1. story eligible' (or reason-string) line. For this one defect class the agreement property is proven only as 'it also fails', which is exactly what ac-3's text forbids relying on.\",\"confidence\":0.4},{\"id\":\"pf-3\",\"text\":\"ac-2's non-mutation evidence is claimed as 'a working-tree diff/git status assertion proving zero mutation', but snapshotRepo (closepreflight_test.go:183-189) captures only HEAD, branch, branch list, and `git status --porcelain`. Porcelain names untracked files but not their contents, so a preflight code path that rewrote an existing untracked file — e.g. the verdicts.json files the fixtures write under .verdi/data/derived/ after the commit, precisely the tree the fold reads — would leave the snapshot byte-identical and pass every non-mutation assertion. The implementation is in fact read-only, but the test's 'proving zero mutation' claim is weaker than stated in the one directory preflight actually touches.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"pf-4\",\"text\":\"ac-1's dc-1 clause says the guard disclosure prints 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'. In runPreflight (closepreflight.go:96-109) a gate error returns exit 2 before the disclosure line is reached, so an operational-error run outside CI without --force-local never prints it. This matches the clause's intent (there is no 'ready' verdict to mistake), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever' to the two verdict outcomes, undisclosed in the spec or code comments.\",\"confidence\":0.15},{\"id\":\"pf-5\",\"text\":\"ac-3 says the agreement pair holds 'for each defect class named in ac-1', and ac-1's feature list includes spec-stale and pending-supersession ('the same two'). Those two classes are exercised as preflight/close pairs only at story scope (closepreflight_test.go); the feature-scope tests cover floor-unmet, unreconciled-stub, and open-implementing-story only. The conditions are the literally shared checkSpecStaleCondition/checkPendingSupersessionCondition functions, so drift is structurally unlikely, but the feature-scope wiring of those two conditions (including printSpecStalePathIfFailing's path line on a feature ref) is never proven by a fixture pair.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/close-preflight@7a07ee0f4dbb047996b1775731e4f4885be4b6f7, spec/close-preflight@48d98b3a39de475e40b37255bc7ce257ccc21333], digest: sha256:4115ac2c2d4b1ca717d8c229b8f39c0e52e6c3f1333fb931b636f7ae29043ec5, integrity: sha256:e3e5e3f5ca7486a5a7ebc794a2ed2574644490d4b9d4af24fe5e3047739a1c10 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-pf-1** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1's attestation obligation requires every disclosed path be 'produced by the real path-construction helpers … never hand-typed or re-derived independently'. The attestation path honors this (evidence.AttestationPath), but two other disclosed paths are independently re-derived string joins: (a) the derived-tree root — closepreflight.go:190,195 re-joins ".verdi/data/derived/<RefSlug>" instead of sharing foldload.go:36's own inline join, and (b) the deviation-report.md path — closepreflight.go:175 re-joins ".verdi/specs/active/<name>/deviation-report.md" separately from checkSpecStaleCondition's own join (closuregate.go:110, and a third copy in internal/wallbadge/ladder.go:61). Both copies currently match byte-for-byte, but nothing structural forces agreement — the exact 'two predicates that can drift' pattern dc-1's second judge fix eliminated for the CI-guard disclosure ('one predicate, two print sites') is left open here: if the fold's read location or the gate's report path ever moves, preflight would keep naming the stale path while the tests (which hand-join the same expectation strings) still pass. (confidence 0.45) — Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the two disclosed diagnostic paths match byte-for-byte (judge admits); no wrong output. Pre-existing and WIDESPREAD, not introduced by this fix: the derived-tree join recurs at ~8 cmd/verdi sites (baseline/foldload/rollup/closefeature/featurematrix/...) and the deviation-report join at ~6 (align/closuregate/gate/disposition/wallbadge); centralizing them into a shared path helper is a cross-cutting code-health refactor beyond this focused three-finding remediation. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.45 exceeds the sub-0.30 accept-expectation — flagged for controller review / a separate code-health pass.
- **judged-pf-2** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 requires each defect-class pair to run both halves 'asserting the refusal reason matches, not merely that it also fails'. The unauthored-attestation-scaffold subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts preflight's disclosure text but on the real-close side asserts only the exit code (gotClose != 1), with no assertion that close's refusal reason is the same eligibility condition — unlike every sibling subtest, which checks the matching '[FAIL] closure: 1. story eligible' (or reason-string) line. For this one defect class the agreement property is proven only as 'it also fails', which is exactly what ac-3's text forbids relying on. (confidence 0.40) — Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the real close DOES refuse for the eligibility reason (an unauthored scaffold is not foldable); only this PRE-EXISTING unauthored subtest's close-side assertion is weaker (exit-code only) than its siblings'. The three NEW ADJ-56 agreement scenarios (findings 1/2/3) each pair the disclosure with close's matching refusal reason, so the dispatch's ac-3 extension mandate is met. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.40 exceeds the sub-0.30 accept-expectation — flagged for controller review.
- **judged-pf-3** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's non-mutation evidence is claimed as 'a working-tree diff/git status assertion proving zero mutation', but snapshotRepo (closepreflight_test.go:183-189) captures only HEAD, branch, branch list, and `git status --porcelain`. Porcelain names untracked files but not their contents, so a preflight code path that rewrote an existing untracked file — e.g. the verdicts.json files the fixtures write under .verdi/data/derived/ after the commit, precisely the tree the fold reads — would leave the snapshot byte-identical and pass every non-mutation assertion. The implementation is in fact read-only, but the test's 'proving zero mutation' claim is weaker than stated in the one directory preflight actually touches. (confidence 0.30) — Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Non-behavioral: the judge confirms the preflight is read-only; only the pre-existing shared snapshotRepo helper's non-mutation proof is weaker (git status --porcelain names untracked files but not their bytes) than ac-2's stated claim. DISCLOSED: confidence 0.30 is AT (not below) the sub-0.30 line — flagged for controller review.
- **judged-pf-4** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1's dc-1 clause says the guard disclosure prints 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'. In runPreflight (closepreflight.go:96-109) a gate error returns exit 2 before the disclosure line is reached, so an operational-error run outside CI without --force-local never prints it. This matches the clause's intent (there is no 'ready' verdict to mistake), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever' to the two verdict outcomes, undisclosed in the spec or code comments. (confidence 0.15) — Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Genuinely-new, non-behavioral, sub-0.30 (0.15): an operational-error (exit 2) run returns before the guard-disclosure line, a literal narrowing of dc-1's 'whenever' that matches its intent (there is no 'ready' verdict to mistake), as the judge concedes.
- **judged-pf-5** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 says the agreement pair holds 'for each defect class named in ac-1', and ac-1's feature list includes spec-stale and pending-supersession ('the same two'). Those two classes are exercised as preflight/close pairs only at story scope (closepreflight_test.go); the feature-scope tests cover floor-unmet, unreconciled-stub, and open-implementing-story only. The conditions are the literally shared checkSpecStaleCondition/checkPendingSupersessionCondition functions, so drift is structurally unlikely, but the feature-scope wiring of those two conditions (including printSpecStalePathIfFailing's path line on a feature ref) is never proven by a fixture pair. (confidence 0.20) — Controller adjudication ADJ-56 (2026-07-17), closing clause. Genuinely-new, non-behavioral, sub-0.30 (0.20): feature-scope spec-stale / pending-supersession lack a dedicated fixture pair, but they are the literally shared checkSpecStaleCondition / checkPendingSupersessionCondition proven as preflight/close pairs at story scope, so the two surfaces are structurally prevented from drifting (dc-2).
